name: Hyperapp
description: Ultra-lightweight 1KB JavaScript framework for building hypertext applications with virtual DOM

description.value: Ultra-lightweight 1KB JavaScript framework for building hypertext applications with virtual DOM

website.comment: No dedicated website; GitHub README serves as primary documentation

latest-release.comment: Version 2.0.0 released July 26, 2019; latest minor is 2.0.22

active-maintenance.comment: Repository shows commits in January 2025; however last npm publish was 4 years ago

reactivity-model.comment: Ultra-lightweight Virtual DOM with highly-optimized diff algorithm

component-model.comment: Uses pure functions for views; stateless components are framework-agnostic functions

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative API that's easy to read and fun to write

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in state management with actions, effects, and subscriptions

opinionatedness.comment: Enforces a specific architecture with state, actions, effects, and subscriptions pattern similar to Elm

backward-compatibility.comment: V1 to V2 had significant breaking changes; V2 removed built-in hydration support

compiles-to.comment: Native JavaScript framework, no compilation required

typescript-support.comment: TypeScript definitions included; can be written with TypeScript using JSX

template-syntax.comment: Uses h() and text() functions; JSX can be used with build step, or hyperscript without

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling solution; use external CSS or any CSS approach

build-required.comment: Can be used via CDN without build step; JSX requires build step

wasm-interop.comment: Pure JavaScript framework, no WASM involvement

reactive-primitives.comment: Uses centralized state object rather than reactive primitives like signals

computed-values.comment: No built-in computed/derived state; compute in actions or views

two-way-binding.comment: One-way data flow; form inputs handled via actions

event-handling.comment: Declarative event handling in view functions

conditional-rendering.comment: Standard JavaScript conditionals (ternary, &&) in view functions

list-rendering.comment: Standard JavaScript array methods (map) in view functions

component-slots.comment: Children passed as arguments to view functions

suspense.comment: No built-in suspense; async handled via effects and subscriptions

context-provide-inject.comment: Uses centralized state; no context/provide-inject pattern needed

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Encourages declarative patterns; DOM access discouraged by design

official-router.comment: hyperrouter exists but is community-maintained; not officially bundled

router-name.comment: Community router; also hyperapp-router for V1

dynamic-routes.comment: Parameterized routes supported via community router

route-guards.comment: RouteAction runs on every push/pop state for navigation control

lazy-loading-routes.comment: No built-in lazy loading for routes

built-in-state.comment: Core feature: state is a plain JavaScript object that describes entire program

built-in-global-state.comment: Single centralized state by design; no separate global state needed

global-state-library.comment: Built-in global state; no external library needed or recommended

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Community devtools available via hyperapp-devtools or hyperapp-redux-devtools

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; effects can be used for localStorage integration

time-travel-debugging.comment: Available via hyperapp-debug browser extension with Elm-inspired time-travel

official-cli.comment: No official CLI; use any bundler or CDN directly

hot-module-replacement.comment: No built-in HMR; depends on bundler configuration

browser-devtools.comment: Community extensions exist (hyperapp-debug) but no official devtools

error-messages.comment: Minimal framework with basic error messages; no special error handling

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate docs with tutorial, examples, and API reference in GitHub; no dedicated docs site

interactive-tutorial.comment: No interactive tutorial; text-based tutorial in repository docs

playground.comment: No official playground; can use CodePen or CodeSandbox

bundle-size-full.comment: Approximately 3.5KB with router; framework is intentionally minimal

virtual-dom.comment: Ultra-lightweight Virtual DOM with highly-optimized diff algorithm

code-splitting.comment: No built-in code splitting; depends on bundler

ssr-capable.comment: SSR re-hydration available; V2 moved hydration to separate package

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Historical benchmark score ~54 (2020); not consistently included in recent benchmarks

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Approximately 5,300 weekly downloads; last publish 4 years ago

component-libraries.comment: No major UI component libraries; hyperawesome collection has community components

major-companies-using.comment: Small-scale production usage; won FreightWaves best in show with Hyperapp apps

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven; maintained by Jorge Bucaran with sponsor support

discord-community-size.comment: Uses Slack for community communication; size unknown

job-market-demand.comment: Niche framework; very few job postings specifically for Hyperapp

official-testing-library.comment: Community testing library (hyperapp-test) available but not official

testing-library-name.comment: Community library; also cypress-hyperapp-unit-test for component testing

component-testing.comment: Pure functions are easy to test; cypress-hyperapp-unit-test available

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Cypress integration available via community package

snapshot-testing.comment: Pure view functions can be snapshot tested with any testing framework

mobile-framework.comment: No official mobile framework; can be used with Capacitor for hybrid apps

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA support; can be manually configured

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop framework; can be used with Electron/Tauri

learning-curve.comment: Minimal concepts: state, view, actions, effects, subscriptions; Elm-inspired but simpler

concepts-to-learn.comment: Core concepts: state, view, actions, effects, subscriptions

time-to-hello-world.comment: 5 minutes; can start immediately with CDN, no build setup required

migration-path.comment: No official migration guides from other frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: Can be added to existing apps via CDN; mounts to specific DOM node
